medications: Naproxen 500 mg as needed for pain/inflammation
Diclofenac 1% topical gel for shoulder pain
Omeprazole 20 mg daily as needed for reflux
Melatonin 3 mg at bedtime as needed
health.mental_health: Grief with intermittent anxiety and sleep disruption since the recent death of a close family member; has started short-term bereavement counseling and is avoiding alcohol most weeks due to feeling emotionally “raw.”
recent_visits: Orthopedic follow-up for ACL rehab progress (sports medicine clinic in Coral Gables)
Physical therapy session for knee strengthening and shoulder mobility (2x/week)
Primary care visit for insomnia and reflux flare-ups (discussed stress management)

finance.debt: Car payment and remaining medical bills from surgery/physical therapy; recently paid for travel and shared funeral expenses for a close family member, which temporarily tightened her monthly budget.
recent_transactions: Round-trip flight and last-minute hotel booking for family funeral services
Payment to funeral home for shared expenses
Automatic transfer to help an older relative cover household bills for a month
Purchase of a knee brace and shoulder support tape

relationships: Mother (Leela Nair) — frequent calls; both grieving and occasionally tense about family decisions after the death
Younger brother (Arjun Nair) — supportive but lives out of state; disagreements about estate paperwork and timelines
Partner (Diego Alvarez) — steady support; she worries about leaning on him too much
Closest friend (Marisol Vega) — confidante; accompanies her to walks/quiet outings when she’s having a hard day
Work friend (Tanya Brooks) — covers for her when she needs to step away for calls or appointments
social.social_media_activity: Mostly inactive lately; posted a brief memorial message and has been avoiding comments/DMs. Keeps Instagram private, rarely shares location, and has muted several family group chats that became overwhelming.
private_conversations: Family disagreements about who should manage the deceased relative’s accounts and paperwork
Her fear that her grief is affecting her relationship and work performance
Details about the death and what happened in the final days (shared only with two trusted people)
Discussing whether to pursue therapy/medication for anxiety and sleep
Concerns about a relative asking for money and how to set boundaries

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Fri, typically 8:30 AM–5:30 PM with occasional late evenings for filings and trial prep
regular_appointments: Physical therapy: Tuesdays and Thursdays at 6:30 PM
Orthopedic check-in: every 6–8 weeks (varies by rehab phase)
Weekly call with mother: Sundays at 6:00 PM
upcoming_events: Estate-related meeting with family and a probate attorney (video call) next week
Court filing deadline support for an upcoming motion (end of month)
Follow-up PT re-evaluation for return-to-running plan
Small memorial dinner with close friends (private, no social media) later this month
schedule.daily_routine: Early coffee and a short knee/shoulder mobility routine; commutes to the office, manages case deadlines and client communications, and often takes a mid-afternoon walk to decompress. Evenings alternate between PT, counseling, and handling family paperwork; sleep is inconsistent.

professional.company: Mid-size civil litigation law firm in Downtown Miami (insurance defense and personal injury)
projects: Drafting and organizing discovery responses for a multi-party auto accident case
Preparing deposition summaries and exhibit binders for an upcoming mediation
Coordinating medical record subpoenas and tracking HIPAA authorizations
Managing court deadlines and e-filing in Florida state courts
professional.performance_issues: Since the death in her family, she’s had moments of reduced concentration and has requested a few short-notice schedule adjustments; she’s anxious about making a deadline mistake and has asked a senior paralegal to double-check one filing calendar.
workplace_relationships: Supervising attorney (Michael Rosen, Esq.) — supportive but deadline-driven; she’s careful about how much she shares
Senior paralegal (Janice Patel) — mentor who helps review her calendar during busy weeks
New legal assistant — Ananya is training them but finds it harder while grieving
HR contact — discreetly arranged short-term bereavement leave and flexible hours

hobbies: Beach walks at South Pointe Park (low-impact rehab-friendly)
Yoga/Pilates modifications for knee and shoulder
Cooking South Indian comfort food (especially on hard days)
Reading legal thrillers and literary fiction
habits: Tracks rehab exercises and pain levels in a notes app
Keeps a private folder of scanned family documents related to the death (insurance, accounts, certificates)
Occasionally takes late-night drives when she can’t sleep
Limits alcohol; has increased caffeine slightly during stressful work stretches
Avoids certain songs/places that trigger intense grief
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a one-bedroom apartment in Miami (Brickell-adjacent); partner stays over several nights a week. Recently cleared a small shelf for a memorial photo and keeps family paperwork in a locked drawer.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly pescatarian with a focus on home-cooked meals; avoids heavy spicy foods when reflux flares; tries to prioritize protein and hydration to support rehab.
